text: {"challenges":[{"challengeType":"missing-source","targetKind":"claim","targetIndex":0,"topic":"official IOC complaint document or public statement","rationale":"The claim that the IOC has received a complaint about Infantino’s political neutrality lacks any cited source, making it impossible to verify the existence or content of the complaint.","suggestedRemedy":"Provide the actual IOC complaint filing, press release, or a reliable news report that documents the complaint."},{"challengeType":"source-does-not-support","targetKind":"claim","targetIndex":5,"topic":"FairSquare article on Infantino neutrality rules","rationale":"The cited FairSquare source is used to assert that the IOC can expel members who violate neutrality rules, but the article does not explicitly state the IOC’s expulsion authority; it only discusses ethical expectations.","suggestedRemedy":"Locate a source that directly confirms the IOC’s power to expel members for breaches of the Olympic Charter or replace the claim with a more accurate statement about the IOC’s disciplinary mechanisms."},{"challengeType":"scope-drift","targetKind":"inference","targetIndex":0,"topic":"","rationale":"Inference [0] treats the fact that Balogun played after alleged lobbying as factual context for the complaint, but the mere occurrence of the match does not substantively support the existence or seriousness of a political‑interference complaint.","suggestedRemedy":"Either remove the inference or replace it with evidence that directly links the lobbying attempt to the complaint’s substance."},{"challengeType":"omitted-qualification","targetKind":"inference","targetIndex":2,"topic":"","rationale":"Inference [2] assumes that the autonomy of FIFA’s judicial committees automatically limits the IOC’s ability to enforce neutrality rules, ignoring the IOC’s separate jurisdiction and any agreements that may allow it to intervene.","suggestedRemedy":"Add a qualification explaining the legal relationship between FIFA’s committees and the IOC, or provide evidence showing the limits of IOC enforcement in this context."}]}
